title: T-124 — UI: D365 Wizard modal — steps 1-4 (d365_wizard_modal first half) — slice of T-046
prompt:
# T-124 — D365 Wizard modal steps 1-4 (slice of T-046)

PRD: docs/prd/01-NPD-PRD.md §10.6.1 + UX d365_wizard_modal
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira
Prototype: prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/npd/modals.jsx:431-512 (first half of d365_wizard_modal)
Parent root: T-046

## Goal
Client Component scaffolding the 8-step D365 Wizard modal with step navigation (Back/Next/Cancel) and rendering steps 1-4: validate, bom_preview, allergen_check, constants_check. Per-step validation gates Next. Matches the first half of the d365_wizard_modal prototype 1:1.

## Prototype reference
**Source:** `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/npd/modals.jsx:431-512`
- `window.Modal + Stepper` → Dialog max-w-4xl; Stepper → custom StepIndicator on shadcn primitives
- 8-step state machine with step/done Set → useReducer with step FSM; each step renders a separate sub-component for testability (this slice implements 1-4)
- BOM preview, allergen, N+1 tables → pre-fetched in parallel via Promise.all in RSC before modal opens; passed as props
- D365 constants table → query d365_constants in RSC; render read-only in step 5 (note: step 5 lives in T-125)

## Implementation contract
New `apps/web/app/(npd)/_modals/d365-wizard-modal-steps-1-4.tsx` 'use client'. Exposes the wizard shell (Stepper + Back/Next/Cancel) and renders sub-components for steps 1-4. Each step calls its server action for validation; Next is disabled until the step is validated. Steps 5-8 will be implemented by T-125 and rendered by the same parent shell or composed via a shared StepFSM.

## Files in scope
- `apps/web/app/(npd)/_modals/d365-wizard-modal-steps-1-4.tsx [create]`
- `apps/web/app/(npd)/_modals/__tests__/d365-wizard-steps-1-4.test.tsx [create]`
- `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/npd/modals.jsx [ref]`

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given the wizard renders, when compared to modals.jsx:431-512, then it shows the same Stepper (8 step labels visible) with Back/Next/Cancel buttons in same positions, and steps 1-4 render their sub-components matching the prototype — verified by RTL parity checklist.
2. Given the user is on step N (N∈1..4), when validation for that step fails, then Next is disabled and an inline error explains why.
3. Given the user is on step N (N∈1..4), when Next is pressed after successful validation, then the wizard advances to step N+1 and the previous step is marked done in the Stepper.
4. UI closeout includes screenshot/artifact evidence and Playwright trace/artifacts where applicable per `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md`.

## Test strategy (RED first)
- RED: 3 RTL tests covering: (a) parity (8 step labels + Back/Next/Cancel + steps 1-4 sub-components), (b) Next disabled on invalid step, (c) advance + done mark on valid step.
- Run: `pnpm --filter web test d365-wizard-steps-1-4`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not implement steps 5-8 here (T-125)
- Do not implement SSE / execute phase here (T-125)
- Do not implement the Build modal in this slice (T-123)
- Do not wire into FA detail here (T-126)
- Do not render plain HTML <input> in any step
